ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Overview
Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management ("CRM") technology that brings companies and their customers together. Founded in 1999, we enable companies of every size and industry to take advantage of powerful technologies to connect to their customers in a whole new way and help them transform their businesses around the customer in this digital-first world.
Our Customer 360 platform unites sales, service, marketing, commerce and IT teams by connecting customer data across systems, apps and devices to create a complete view of customers. With this single source of customer truth, teams can be more responsive, productive and efficient, deliver intelligent, personalized experiences across every channel and increase productivity. With Slack, we provide a digital headquarters where companies, employees, governments and stakeholders can collaborate to create success from anywhere.
Our Customer 360 service offerings are designed to be flexible, scalable and easy to use. They can generally be configured easily, deployed rapidly and integrated with other platforms and enterprise applications. We sell to businesses worldwide, primarily on a subscription basis, through our direct sales efforts and also indirectly through partners. We also enable third parties to use our platform and developer tools to create additional functionality and new applications that run on our platform, which are sold separately from, or in conjunction with, our service offerings.
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. Foremost among these is trust, which is the foundation for everything we do. Our customers trust our technology to deli
…[truncated 20261 chars]…
consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
8
Table of Contents
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new apps that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the accelerated shift to cloud and hosted service offerings and customer experience management solutions. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than five percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2023, 2022 or 2021. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-cloud and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours at no charge to customers who purchase any of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incidence response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific enterprise architecture.
Sales and Marketing
We sell our services primarily through our direct sales force, which comprises telephone sales personnel based in regional hubs, field sales personnel based in territories close to their customers and self-service offerings.
To a lesser extent, we also utilize a network of partners who refer sales leads to us and assist in selling to these prospects. This network includes global consulting firms, systems integrators and other partners. In return, we typically pay these partners a fee based on the first-year subscription revenue generated by the customers whom they refer. We continue to invest in developing additional distribution channels for our subscription services.
We use a variety of marketing programs across traditional and social channels to target our prospective and current customers, partners and developers. We focus our marketing activities in the cities and countries with the largest market opportunities. Our primary marketing activities include:
- multichannel marketing campaigns that span email, social media, the web, television and more, which align to a broader customer journey;
- in-person and virtual customer events of all sizes to create customer and prospect awareness, including proprietary events such as Dreamforce and our virtual Dreamforce to You, World Tours and other virtual events, as well as participation in trade shows and industry events;
- live events and original programming on our Salesforce+ streaming service, which includes discussions about the future of technology in the digital-first, work anywhere world and educational content to learn new skills and pursue new career opportunities;
- press and industry analyst relations to garner third-party validation and generate positive coverage for our company, brand, service offerings and value proposition;
- partner co-marketing activities with global and regional implementation partners;
- customer testimonials and our community of Trailblazers: individuals who drive innovation, grow their careers and transform their businesses using the Customer 360 platform;
- in-person and virtual technology event sponsorships; and
- event partnerships with high-profile global brands and organizations.
Intellectual Property
We rely on a combination of trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets and patents and contractual provisions, to protect our proprietary technology and our brands. We also enter into confidentiality and proprietary rights agreements with our employees, consultants and other third parties and control access to software, services, documentation and other proprietary information. We believe the duration of our patents is adequate relative to the expected lives of our service offerings. We also purchase or
9
Table of Contents
license technology that we incorporate into our products or services. At times, we make select intellectual property broadly available at no or low cost to achieve a strategic objective, such as promoting industry standards, advancing interoperability, supporting open source software or attracting and enabling our external development community. While it may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of our products and business methods, we believe, based upon past experience and industry practice, such licenses generally could be obtained on commercially reasonable terms.
Human Capital Management
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. These core values are the foundation of our company culture, which we believe is fundamental to, and a competitive advantage in, our approach to managing our workforce. We believe our company culture fosters open dialogue, collaboration, recognition and a sense of family, all of which allow us to attract and retain the best talent, which is critical for our continued success. For example, our sales, engineering and customer success teams are critical to our ability to grow, innovate and ensure the trust and customer success of our customers.
We believe our efforts in managing our workforce have been effective. Our focus on our workplace environment and a strong company culture has led to recognition across the globe, as evidenced by the following awards: Fortune World's Most Admired Companies (2022 and for the eighth year in a row), Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For (2022 and for the 13th year in a row), Human Rights Campaign Best Places to Work for LGBTQ Equality (2022) and Glassdoor Employees' Choice Best Place to Work in Canada, France, Germany, the United Kingdom and the United States (2022).
As of January 31, 2023, we had 79,390 employees, of which approximately 52 percent were located in the United States and 48 percent were located internationally and approximately 36 percent identified as women, 63 percent identified as men and less than 0.2 percent identified as non-binary. This number includes those individuals impacted by our restructuring plan announced on January 4, 2023, which includes a reduction of our current workforce by approximately 10 percent. None of our employees in the United States are represented by a labor union. However, employees of certain foreign subsidiaries are represented by works councils.

CRM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: CRM (as of 2024-04-01, evidence point-in-time before date)**

1.  **Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
    *   **Revenue:** Trajectory is positive. Latest quarterly revenue (2024-01-31 Q4) is **$9.29B**, representing QoQ growth of **+6.5%** and YoY growth of **+10.8%**. TTM revenue is **$34.86B**.
    *   **Earnings & Margins:** Earnings have rebounded dramatically. Latest quarterly net income is **$1.45B** with EPS diluted of **$1.47**, a QoQ increase of **+17.6%**. This follows several quarters of low profitability (e.g., EPS of **$0.03** in 2022-04-30 and **-$0.10** in 2023-01-31). The gross margin (latest quarterly) is **~76.9%** (from **$7.14B** gross profit on **$9.29B** revenue). The operating margin improved to **~17.4%** (from **$1.62B** operating income) from a low of **~0.3%** in 2022-04-30. TTM net income is **$4.14B**.
    *   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity is **$59.65B** against total debt of **$9.43B**, yielding a debt-to-equity ratio of **0.158**. Total assets are **$99.82B**. Equity has been stable, fluctuating between **$57.41B** (2023-04-30) and **$60.10B** (2022-07-31).

2.  **Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
    *   **Earnings Quality:** The YoY EPS growth of **-1570.0%** is an artifact of comparing a profitable quarter (Q4 2024) to a prior-quarter loss (Q4 2023, EPS **-$0.10**). The recent quarterly EPS trend (**$0.03, $0.07, $0.21, -$0.10, $0.20, $1.28, $1.25, $1.47**) shows a pronounced acceleration in the last three quarters. Net margin (TTM) is **11.9%** (from valuation ratios), up from **0.7%** in April 2023.
    *   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The low debt-to-equity ratio of **0.158** indicates a conservative capital structure. The equity base is substantial at **$59.65B**.
    *   **Cash Flow:** **Not available** for any period in the supplied evidence. This is a critical data gap for assessing true cash generation and earnings conversion.

3.  **Valuation vs. History**
    *   Latest P/E diluted is **71.71**, which is at the lower end of its 1-year range of **70.1..1063.7**. The P/B ratio is **4.969**, within its 1-year range of **3.2..5.4**.
    *   Over the past year, the P/E has compressed significantly as earnings have grown (e.g., from **935.67** on 2023-04-03). The current P/E remains elevated, but the decline reflects improved earnings power rather than price weakness alone. The P/S ratio is **8.502**.

4.  **Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
    *   **Price Level:** The stock closed at **$301.18** on 2024-03-28, below its 1-year high of **$318.71** and well above its low of **$187.31**.
    *   **Returns:** The annual price return is **+56.6%** and the quarterly return is **+12.9%**. The price path shows a strong recovery from mid-2023 (**$202.73** in Sep 2023) to present.
    *   **Fundamental Context:** The price appreciation has coincided with and been supported by the marked acceleration in quarterly earnings and margins beginning in Q2 2023. The stock has re-rated from an extreme high-multiple (loss-based) valuation to a high-multiple growth valuation as earnings materialized.

5.  **Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
    *   **Upside:** The strongest quantitative upside is the demonstrated and recent acceleration in revenue growth (to **+10.8%** YoY) and operating leverage, with operating margin expanding to **~17.4%** from sub-**1%** levels. If sustained, this earnings growth trajectory could justify the current valuation.
    *   **Downside:** The strongest quantitative downside is the **absence of cash flow data**, leaving earnings quality unverified. Furthermore, the current P/E of **71.71** prices in substantial continued growth; any deceleration in revenue growth or margin compression could lead to significant multiple contraction.

6.  **Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
    *   The fundamental trajectory is firmly positive, with accelerating revenue and expanding profitability. The balance sheet is resilient with low leverage.
    *   However, the valuation (P/E **71.71**) is historically high and demands continued execution on the growth and margin expansion seen in recent quarters.
    *   The significant price appreciation (+56.6% annual) has largely reflected these improving fundamentals, leaving the risk/reward dependent on the durability of this financial improvement. The critical lack of cash flow data introduces an unquantified risk to earnings quality. The quantitative case is positive on trajectory but caution is warranted by the valuation premium and the cash flow evidence gap.
CRM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: CRM (Salesforce)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM) technology, providing an integrated Customer 360 platform that unifies sales, service, marketing, commerce, IT, and collaboration (via Slack) functions. The business operates on a multi-tenant cloud architecture, selling subscriptions primarily through a direct sales force to businesses of all sizes across numerous industries globally. Its strategy centers on expanding existing customer relationships through cross-selling, increasing geographic and industry-specific penetration, and leveraging a partner ecosystem. The company values trust, customer success, and innovation as core differentiators.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **2024-03-08:** Analysis notes expected EPS growth of 18% YoY driven by cost containment. The company declared its first-ever quarterly dividend ($0.40/share) and increased its share buyback program by $10 billion.
*   **2024-03-05 & 2024-02-29:** Reports highlight strong Q4 and full-year 2024 results, fueled by "strong sales of Data Cloud" (referred to as the "fastest-growing" product). An analyst affirmed a Buy rating based on solid performance and a strong outlook for profitability and demand.
*   **2024-03-07:** A review notes Salesforce is considered the top CRM provider for the 10th consecutive year by IDC, reinforcing its market leadership.
*   **2024-03-23:** An investment analysis article downgraded the stock to a Hold rating, arguing the current price (~$305) is "overvalued" with limited upside, representing a more cautious near-term view.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk - Active Litigation:** The company faces significant class-action lawsuits. (1) A **2024-03-21** report states a judge refused to dismiss a 50,000-person class action alleging poor management of its 401(k) plan, with the case headed toward trial. (2) A **2024-03-29** report notes a lawsuit alleging Salesforce participated in a sex trafficking scheme, though the federal judge dismissed some claims based on immunity law; the core case appears to persist.
*   **Risk - Intense Competition:** The 10-K filing explicitly describes the market as "highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented...with low barriers to entry," listing competitors ranging from packaged software vendors to productivity suite providers.
*   **Offset - Strong Product Demand & Market Position:** Recent results (cited **2024-03-05**) show robust demand, particularly for high-growth products like Data Cloud. Sustained recognition as the #1 CRM vendor by IDC for a decade (cited **2024-03-07**) underscores a durable market position.
*   **Offset - Capital Return & Financial Discipline:** The company has initiated shareholder returns via a new dividend and a materially increased buyback program (cited **2024-03-08**), signaling management confidence in cash flow generation and a commitment to shareholder value.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The franchise remains strong as the undisputed market leader in CRM, evidenced by sustained analyst recognition and recent financial results highlighting robust demand for its integrated platform. Strategic initiatives to improve profitability and return capital provide clear shareholder value offsets to the material litigation risks. While legal overhang and competitive pressures are non-trivial, the core business narrative of market leadership and innovation appears intact based on the latest available evidence.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'operating margin improved to ~17.4% (from $1.62B operating income)' for the latest quarter. The raw evidence shows operating_income=1.62B on revenue=9.29B, which yields ~17.4%. However, the valuation ratios panel shows operating_margin=0.144 (14.4%) as the latest figure. The quant report uses the quarterly calculation (~17.4%) while the valuation ratio panel shows 14.4% — these are likely different measurement windows (quarterly vs. TTM or trailing). The report should clarify which period the 17.4% refers to and note the discrepancy with the 14.4% valuation ratio figure.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the ~17.4% operating margin is a single-quarter figure (Q4 2024) while the 14.4% in the valuation ratios likely reflects a trailing or different measurement window. No score change needed, but the inconsistency should be noted for the PM.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'The YoY EPS growth of -1570.0% is an artifact of comparing a profitable quarter (Q4 2024) to a prior-quarter loss (Q4 2023, EPS -$0.10).' The raw evidence confirms Q4 2023 (2023-01-31) EPS was -0.10 and Q4 2024 (2024-01-31) EPS was 1.47. The -1570% figure is correctly explained as a base-effect artifact. This is accurate and well-handled.
corrections.required_action: No action needed — verified as accurate.

corrections.issue: The qual report cites 'strong sales of Data Cloud' and that CEO called it 'the fastest-growing product.' The raw qualitative evidence from the 2024-03-05 Futurum article confirms this language ('strong sales of Data Cloud, which CEO Marc Benioff called the fastest gr[owing]'). The qual report also states Salesforce is '#1 CRM provider for the 10th consecutive year by IDC' — the 2024-03-07 Yahoo Finance article in the raw evidence confirms this. These claims are supported.
corrections.required_action: No action needed — verified as accurate.

unsupported_claims: : The quant report's ~17.4% operating margin for Q4 2024 is arithmetically correct from the raw income statement (1.62B / 9.29B), but the valuation ratios panel shows operating_margin=0.144 (14.4%) without explanation of the measurement difference. This is not an unsupported claim per se, but an unexplained discrepancy between two figures in the same evidence pack that the report does not address.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Salesforce has delivered a genuine profitability inflection with Q4 FY24 operating margin of ~17.4% and EPS of $1.47, but the stock's +56.6% annual return has already priced in much of this improvement at a P/E of 71.7x. The risk/reward is balanced—strong fundamentals and market leadership offset by elevated valuation and an unverified cash flow picture.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Salesforce has delivered a genuine profitability inflection with Q4 FY24 operating margin of ~17.4% and EPS of $1.47, but the stock's +56.6% annual return has already priced in much of this improvement at a P/E of 71.7x. The risk/reward is balanced—strong fundamentals and market leadership offset by elevated valuation and an unverified cash flow picture.
key_factors: Profitability inflection is real and accelerating: Q4 FY24 net income of $1.45B with EPS of $1.47, up from -$0.10 a year ago, and operating margin expanding to ~17.4% from sub-1% levels in 2022.
Valuation remains demanding at P/E of 71.7x and P/S of 8.5x, pricing in continued execution on growth and margin expansion after a +56.6% annual price return.

Revenue growth re-accelerated to +10.8% YoY in Q4 FY24 to $9.29B, with Data Cloud cited as the fastest-growing product, supporting the franchise's market leadership (#1 CRM by IDC for 10 consecutive years).

Critical cash flow data is absent from the evidence, leaving earnings quality and cash conversion unverified—a material gap given the elevated multiple and the company's new dividend initiation and $10B buyback expansion.

Item 1. BUSINESS
Amgen Inc. (including its subsidiaries, referred to as "Amgen," "the Company," "we," "our" or "us") is a biotechnology company committed to unlocking the potential of biology for patients suffering from serious illnesses by discovering, developing, manufacturing and delivering innovative human therapeutics. This approach begins by using tools like advanced human genetics to unravel the complexities of disease and understand the fundamentals of human biology.
Amgen focuses on areas of high unmet medical need and leverages its expertise to strive for solutions that improve health outcomes and dramatically improve people's lives. A biotechnology pioneer, Amgen has grown to be one of the world's leading independent biotechnology companies, has reached millions of patients around the world and is developing a pipeline of medicines with breakaway potential.
Amgen was incorporated in California in 1980 and became a Delaware corporation in 1987. We have a presence in approximately 100 countries worldwide. Amgen operates in one business segment: human therapeutics.
Significant Developments
Following is a summary of significant developments affecting our business that have occurred and that we have reported since the filing of our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended Dece
…[truncated 20710 chars]…
e New Jersey District Court. We disagree with the court's holding and we are in the process of appealing this judgment. See Part IV—Note 19, Contingencies and commitments, to the Consolidated Financial Statements, Amgen Inc. v. Sandoz Inc., et al.
(3)A patent with this subject matter may be entitled to patent term extension in the United States.
7
Competition
We operate in a highly competitive environment. A number of our marketed products are indicated for disease areas in which other products or treatments are currently available or are being pursued by our competitors through R&D activities. Additionally, some competitor-marketed products target the same genetic pathways as our recently launched marketed products or are currently in development. This competition could impact the pricing and market share of our products. We continue to pursue ways of increasing the value of our medicines through innovations during their life cycles, which can include expanding the disease areas for which our products are indicated and finding new methods to make the delivery of our medicines easier and less costly. Such activities can offer important opportunities for differentiation. For example, we market the Neulasta Onpro kit, which provides physicians the opportunity to initiate administration of the recommended dose of Neulasta on the same day as chemotherapy, with drug delivery at home the day after chemotherapy, thereby saving the patient a trip back to the doctor. We plan to continue pursuing innovation efforts to strengthen our competitive position. Such position may be based on, among other things, safety, efficacy, reliability, availability, patient convenience, delivery devices, price, reimbursement, access to and timing of market entry and patent position and expiration.
Certain of the existing patents on our principal products have expired, and we face new and increasing competition, including from biosimilars and generics. A biosimilar is another version of a biological product for which marketing approval is sought or has been obtained based on a demonstration that it is "highly similar" to the original reference product. We have experienced adverse effects from biosimilar competition on our originator product sales. Companies have launched biosimilar versions of EPOGEN, NEUPOGEN and Neulasta and have approved biosimilars for ENBREL. Once multiple biosimilar versions of one of our originator products have launched, competition has intensified rapidly, resulting in greater net price declines for both reference and biosimilar products and a greater effect on product sales. See also Government Regulation—Regulation in the United States—Approval of Biosimilars. Although competitor biosimilars compete on price, we believe many patients, providers and payers will continue to place high value on the reputation, supply reliability and safety of our products. As additional biosimilar competitors come to market, we will continue to leverage our global experience to distinguish against both branded and biosimilar competition.
We also have our own biosimilar products both in the United States and outside of U.S. markets that are competing against branded and biosimilar versions of our competitors' products. In 2019, Amgen launched MVASI, a biosimilar to Avastin, and KANJINTI, a biosimilar to Herceptin; and in 2018, Amgen launched AMGEVITA, a biosimilar to HUMIRA in markets outside the United States. We have also received FDA approval of AMJEVITA, a biosimilar to HUMIRA for the U.S. market, which launched in January 2023. In 2020, we launched AVSOLA, a biosimilar to Remicade; and in 2021 we launched RIABNI, a biosimilar to Rituxan. We expect additional biosimilar competition against both our branded and biosimilar products in the future across markets.
Although most of our products are biologics, some are small molecule products. Because the FDA approval process permits generic manufacturers to rely on the safety and efficacy data of the innovator product rather than having to conduct their own costly and time-consuming clinical trials, generic manufacturers can often develop and market their competing versions of our small molecule products at much lower prices. For example, following loss of exclusivity of patents directed to cinacalcet, the active ingredient in our small molecule calcimimetic Sensipar, we lost a significant share of the market and corresponding revenues in a very short period of time. See Part IV—Note 19, Contingencies and commitments, to the Consolidated Financial Statements.
The introduction of new products, the development of new processes or technologies by competitors or the emergence of new information about existing products may result in (i) increased competition for our marketed products, even for those protected by patents and/or (ii) reductions in the prices we receive from selling our products. In addition, the development of new treatment options or standards of care may reduce the use of our products or may limit the utility and application of ongoing clinical trials of our product candidates. (As used in this document, the term clinical trials may include prospective clinical trials, observational studies, registries and other studies.) See Item 1A. Risk Factors—Our products face substantial competition and our product candidates are also likely to face substantial competition and Item 1A. Risk Factors—We currently face competition from biosimilars and generics and expect to face increasing competition from biosimilars and generics in the future.

AMGN.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: AMGN as of 2024-04-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** TTM revenue is $28.19B. Q4 2023 revenue of $8.20B shows strong QoQ growth of +18.7% and YoY growth of +19.8%.
*   **Earnings & Margins:** TTM net income is $6.72B. However, Q4 2023 net income collapsed to $767.00M, a QoQ decrease of -55.7% and YoY decrease of -52.5%. The Q4 net margin was only 9.35% (Net Income/Revenue), far below prior quarters' range of 21.3% to 46.5%. TTM gross profit is $19.74B, with a gross margin of ~69.1% (Gross Profit/Revenue), but Q4 2023 cost of revenue surged to $3.11B, compressing the Q4 gross margin to 61.95%.
*   **Cash Flow:** Quarterly cash flow data is unavailable.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew to $97.15B in Q4 2023 from $65.12B a year prior. Total equity is $6.23B. Total debt is $64.61B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The Q4 2023 earnings collapse is severe, with EPS of $1.43 versus Q3's $3.22, despite higher revenue. This severe decoupling suggests poor earnings quality in the period, likely driven by non-operating items or charges.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet is highly leveraged. The debt-to-equity ratio is 10.368. Total debt of $64.61B is over 10x the equity base of $6.23B. This leverage is a primary risk factor.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
*   As of 2024-03-28, the P/E (diluted) is 22.764. This is near the upper bound of its 365-day range (14.6 to 23.2). The P/B ratio is 24.545, within its 365-day range of 18.0 to 37.4. The earnings yield is 4.4%. The valuation is not low; the P/E has expanded after a period of contraction and is at a premium given the deteriorating recent earnings trajectory.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   The stock closed at $284.32 on 2024-03-28. The quarterly return (since 2023-12-27) is -0.8%, while the annual return (since 2023-03-28) is +19.2%. The price is in the mid-to-lower portion of its 365-day range (high $329.72, low $211.71). The positive annual return contrasts with the deterioration in Q4 2023 profit margins and earnings.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Strong TTM revenue growth (+$28.19B) and recent Q/Q revenue acceleration (+18.7%). The annual price return has been positive (+19.2%).
*   **Downside:** Extreme balance sheet leverage (D/E of 10.368). Severe earnings deterioration in the most recent quarter (EPS -55.6% QoQ, net margin 9.35%). Valuation is not cheap relative to this deteriorating fundamental path (P/E at 22.8).

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative evidence presents a conflicted picture. While revenue growth is robust, the most recent quarter shows a dramatic and concerning degradation in profitability, with earnings cut by over half. This occurred despite higher revenue, indicating severe margin compression. The valuation is not discounted to this new, lower earnings power. The balance sheet is a significant structural risk with leverage over 10x equity. The positive annual price move (+19.2%) may not have fully accounted for the Q4 2023 earnings deterioration. Based on the supplied numbers, the risk profile appears unfavorable: high leverage combined with a sharp, unexplained contraction in margins and earnings, and no compensating valuation discount.
AMGN.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Amgen Inc. (AMGN)**  
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

---

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
From the 2023 10-K filing (filing_date: 2023-02-09), Amgen is a global biotechnology company operating in a single human therapeutics segment. Its franchise is built on a diversified portfolio across cardiometabolic, inflammation, oncology, and bone health therapeutic areas. Key marketed products include Enbrel (autoimmune), Prolia/Xgeva (bone disorders), Repatha (cardiovascular), Otezla (inflammation), and Tezspire (asthma). The company has expanded into rare diseases through its acquisition of Horizon Therapeutics (announced 2022-12-12). Amgen emphasizes innovation, leveraging human genetics, and has a global commercial presence relying heavily on U.S. pharmaceutical wholesalers.

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Pre-2024-04-01)
*   **Financial Guidance & Analyst Views (2024-03-07):** Amgen provided 2024 revenue guidance of $32.4 billion to $33.8 billion, expecting volume-driven growth from products like Repatha, Tezspire, Evenity, Prolia, and Blincyto.
*   **Growth Catalysts & Analyst Coverage (2024-02-26, 2024-03-28):** Goldman Sachs (2024-02-26) noted Amgen has upcoming 2024 catalysts to unlock multi-billion dollar markets, maintaining a Buy rating. Raymond James (2024-03-28) resumed coverage, highlighting Amgen's diversified portfolio reduces concentration risk and that the Horizon acquisition bolsters long-term growth potential.
*   **Rare Disease Potential (2024-02-26):** Amgen expressed confidence in the growth potential of its rare disease franchise, citing that Tepezza penetration is still in the high single digits.
*   **Regulatory/Legal Challenge (2024-03-25 to 2024-03-26):** Amgen filed a lawsuit in Colorado federal court (2024-03-25/26) challenging the state's Prescription Drug Affordability Review Board's plan to impose an "upper payment limit" on Enbrel. The suit argues the board's actions are unconstitutional.
*   **FTC Resolution (2024-03-12):** Analysis of the FTC's settlement with Amgen/Horizon noted the regulator's alleged harms were speculative and that the merged entity would face significant competitive hurdles.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Legal & Reimbursement Pressure:** The active lawsuit against Colorado's drug pricing board (2024-03-25/26) signals significant political and legal risk to pricing power, particularly for mature drugs like Enbrel.
*   **Patent Cliffs & Generic/Biosimilar Competition:** The 10-K details intense competition and notes key patents for major products (e.g., Prolia/Xgeva, Enbrel formulations) are expiring in the mid-to-late 2020s, exposing them to biosimilar erosion.
*   **Wholesaler Concentration:** Sales are highly concentrated with three major distributors, creating a dependency risk.
*   **Post-Merger Integration:** The Horizon acquisition adds complexity and carries integration risk, though the FTC settlement has cleared a major hurdle.

**Offsets:**
*   **Strategic Diversification:** The portfolio is diversified across therapeutic areas, and the Horizon deal specifically targets high-growth, less-penetrated rare disease markets, mitigating single-product reliance.
*   **Documented Growth Drivers:** Company guidance (2024-03-07) and analyst notes (2024-02-26) point to multiple in-line products with volume-driven growth momentum.
*   **Upcoming Pipeline Catalysts:** Analysts identify 2024 as a year with potential catalysts for new, large market opportunities (2024-02-26).
*   **Manufacturing Capacity Investment:** The company is expanding its manufacturing network with a new facility (noted in 10-K), supporting future supply.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.** Amgen's franchise is fundamentally supported by a diversified therapeutic portfolio and strategic expansion into rare diseases via the Horizon acquisition. Positive 2024 revenue guidance and identified near-term pipeline catalysts provide a clear growth narrative. However, this is tempered by material legal risk over drug pricing (Colorado lawsuit) and the long-term overhang of patent expirations and biosimilar competition. The balance of evidence suggests a resilient business model with identifiable growth levers, offset by significant sector-wide headwinds.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Q4 2023 cost of revenue surged to $3.11B, compressing the Q4 gross margin to 61.95%.' The raw data confirms cost_of_revenue=3.11B and gross_profit=5.08B on revenue=8.20B, yielding 5.08/8.20=61.95%. However, the report also says 'TTM gross profit is $19.74B, with a gross margin of ~69.1% (Gross Profit/Revenue).' TTM revenue is $28.19B, so 19.74/28.19=70.0%, not 69.1%. Minor rounding discrepancy but should be corrected to ~70.0%.
corrections.required_action: Correct TTM gross margin from ~69.1% to ~70.0% (19.74/28.19).

corrections.issue: The qual report states 'The 10-K details intense competition and notes key patents for major products (e.g., Prolia/Xgeva, Enbrel formulations) are expiring in the mid-to-late 2020s.' The raw 10-K patent table shows Prolia/Xgeva U.S. patents expiring 11/30/2023 and 2/19/2025 (already expired or imminent), and Enbrel formulation patent expiring 10/19/2037 (not mid-to-late 2020s). The characterization 'mid-to-late 2020s' is imprecise and could mislead on timing. Some patents have already expired (Prolia/Xgeva 11/30/2023, Enbrel methods 6/8/2023).
corrections.required_action: Clarify that some key patents (Prolia/Xgeva, Enbrel methods) have already expired or expire imminently (2023-2025), while Enbrel formulation patent extends to 2037. The patent cliff is more nuanced than 'mid-to-late 2020s.'

corrections.issue: The quant report notes 'The positive annual price move (+19.2%) may not have fully accounted for the Q4 2023 earnings deterioration.' This is a reasonable observation but the report does not note that the Q4 earnings collapse appears driven by the Horizon acquisition (closed ~2023-10-06 based on balance sheet jump from $65B to $97B assets in Q4 2022→Q4 2023, and debt surge from $39B to $65B). The raw balance sheet shows total_assets jumping from $65.12B (2022-12-31) to $97.15B (2023-12-31) and total_debt from $38.95B to $64.61B, consistent with the ~$28.5B financing noted in the 10-K. The Q4 margin compression is likely acquisition-related (amortization, integration costs) rather than organic deterioration, which materially changes the interpretation.
corrections.required_action: Note that the Q4 2023 earnings collapse and margin compression are likely driven by Horizon acquisition-related costs (amortization, integration charges, interest on ~$28.5B acquisition financing), not organic business deterioration. The balance sheet evidence (asset/debt jumps) and 10-K acquisition disclosure support this. This context is critical for interpreting the earnings quality concern.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Amgen presents a balanced risk/reward profile: strong revenue growth and a diversified franchise offset by extreme leverage (D/E ~10.4x), a Q4 2023 earnings collapse likely driven by Horizon acquisition-related costs rather than organic weakness, and a P/E near the top of its historical range at ~22.8x. The stock is fairly valued for a stable, dividend-paying biotech with near-term catalysts, but the leverage and patent cliff risks limit upside.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Amgen presents a balanced risk/reward profile: strong revenue growth and a diversified franchise offset by extreme leverage (D/E ~10.4x), a Q4 2023 earnings collapse likely driven by Horizon acquisition-related costs rather than organic weakness, and a P/E near the top of its historical range at ~22.8x. The stock is fairly valued for a stable, dividend-paying biotech with near-term catalysts, but the leverage and patent cliff risks limit upside.
key_factors: P/E of 22.8x sits at the 96th percentile of its 365-day range (14.6x–23.2), leaving limited room for multiple expansion and exposing the stock to downside if earnings disappoint further.
Debt-to-equity of 10.4x ($64.6B debt vs. $6.2B equity) reflects Horizon acquisition financing and represents extreme financial leverage, amplifying downside risk in a rising-rate environment.

Q4 2023 net income collapsed to $767M (EPS $1.43 vs. Q3's $3.22) with net margin of 9.35%, but this is likely driven by acquisition-related amortization, integration costs, and interest on ~$28.5B in new debt rather than organic business deterioration.

2024 revenue guidance of $32.4B–$33.8B and analyst-identified pipeline catalysts (Goldman Sachs, 2024-02-26) support a volume-driven growth narrative, while the FTC settlement (2024-03-12) has cleared the Horizon regulatory hurdle.

  note: filing is 430d before decision date; newest available upstream under PIT — a more recent annual 10-K may be missing from the data provider.
Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-ro
…[truncated 9031 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact.

Many factors, including those set forth in the "Risk Factors" section below and other important factors disclosed in this report and from time to time in our other filings with the SEC, could cause actual results to differ materially and adversely from these forward-looking statements. BA.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: BA as of 2024-04-01**

**1. Revenue, earnings, margin, cash-flow, and balance-sheet trajectory**  
- Revenue: Q4 2023 was $22.02B, up 21.6% QoQ from $18.10B (Q3 2023) and up 10.2% YoY from $19.98B (Q4 2022). TTM revenue: $77.79B.  
- Earnings: Net income Q4 2023 was -$30.00M (vs. -$1.64B in Q3 2023). EPS diluted: -$0.03 (vs. -$2.70 in Q3). TTM net income: -$2.24B.  
- Margins: Q4 2023 gross margin: 12.3% ($2.70B/$22.02B), operating margin: 1.3% ($283.00M/$22.02B), net margin: -0.3% (-$30.00M/$22.02B). TTM operating income: -$773.00M.  
- Cash flow: Data unavailable for all periods.  
- Balance sheet: As of Q4 2023, total assets $137.01B, total equity -$17.23B, total debt $52.05B. Equity has been negative since at least Q1 2022 (e.g., -$15.27B in Q1 2022).

**2. Earnings quality and balance-sheet resilience**  
- Earnings quality: TTM net income is -$2.24B, with operating losses (-$773.00M TTM). Net margin has been negative across all quarters, indicating poor profitability and low quality.  
- Balance-sheet resilience: Negative equity (-$17.23B) implies liabilities ($154.24B) exceed assets ($137.01B). Total debt of $52.05B represents high leverage (debt-to-asset ratio of 0.38). Low resilience with persistent negative equity.

**3. Valuation versus history**  
- Valuation ratios: P/S ratio is 1.503 as of 2024-03-28. Net margin history shows improvement from -0.076 (April 2023) to -0.029 (March 2024). No P/E, EV/EBIT, or FCF yield data available. Valuation is based on revenue with negative earnings.

**4. Price level and returns versus fundamentals**  
- Price: Latest close $192.99 (2024-03-28). Quarterly return: -26.4% from $262.10 (2023-12-27). Annual return: -5.8% from $204.96 (2023-03-28). 52-week high: $267.54, low: $176.25.  
- Context: Price declined sharply (-26.4% QoQ) despite revenue growth (+21.6% QoQ). Fundamentals show improving margins but persistent losses, with price near the lower end of its range.

**5. Strongest quantitative upside and downside**  
- Upside: Revenue growth trends (QoQ +21.6%, YoY +10.2%) and improving net margins (from -0.076 to -0.029). If margins continue to normalize, earnings could turn positive.  
- Downside: Negative equity (-$17.23B) and high debt ($52.05B). TTM operating loss of -$773.00M and no cash flow data raise liquidity concerns. Risk of further balance-sheet deterioration.

**6. Provisional quantitative risk/reward conclusion**  
Risk is elevated due to negative equity, high leverage, and TTM losses. Potential reward exists from revenue growth and margin recovery, but fundamentals remain challenged. Price decline reflects poor earnings, with valuation contingent on sustained improvement. Neutral to cautious risk/reward balance.
BA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Boeing (BA)**
**Decision Date:** 2024-04-01
**Evidence Cutoff:** 2024-03-30

### 1. Business / Franchise Read
Boeing is a major global aerospace company organized into four segments: Commercial Airplanes (BCA), Defense, Space & Security (BDS), Global Services (BGS), and Boeing Capital (BCC). BCA is a leading producer of commercial jetliners (737, 767, 777, 787 families). BDS competes in military aircraft, space, and systems markets against firms like Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman. BGS provides lifecycle services for both commercial and defense platforms. The business is heavily regulated (FAA, U.S. military) and competitive, with Airbus as a primary rival in commercial aviation and new entrants (e.g., from China) noted (Filing Date: 2023-01-27).

### 2. Recent Developments (Supplied Text Only)
*   **Corporate Actions (2024-03-26):** Significant management overhaul announced. CEO David Calhoun and Chairman Larry Kellner revealed decisions to step down, following a federal investigation into a January 5th incident where a door panel blew out mid-flight. A new CEO for the Commercial Airplanes unit was also named.
*   **Contract Win (2024-03-27):** Boeing secured a $96.2 million contract for the MH-47G helicopter, indicating continued activity in the military helicopter market.
*   **Market & Analyst Sentiment (2024-03-27 to 2024-03-30):** Stock price noted at $192.99 on 2024-03-28. Analyst commentary from Citi and others highlighted potential upside (target of $262 cited) despite acknowledged quality control issues, suggesting a focus on a potential recovery thesis.
*   **Operational & Backlog (Point-in-time from web context):** A 2023 Q4 report (dated 2024-02-13) noted a record backlog of $520 billion. However, the same report referenced $1.6 billion in additional losses on fixed-price development programs.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Intensified Regulatory & Legal Scrutiny.** The January 5th door plug incident has triggered a comprehensive FAA audit of Boeing's 737 MAX 9 production and supply chain (2024-03-21), multiple shareholder lawsuits (2024-03-21, 2024-02-22), and passenger lawsuits (2024-03-04). This follows prior 737 MAX crashes. Additional lawsuits allege harassment and discrimination (2024-03-06).
*   **Risk: Quality Control and Production Concerns.** The investigations and lawsuits allege failures in ensuring aircraft safety compliance with FAA regulations (2024-03-04) and "excessive defects" at a key supplier (2024-03-20). Delivery concerns were noted by analysts (2024-03-26).
*   **Risk: Labor Relations.** Major union contracts are noted: IAM agreements expire in September 2024 and July 2025 (Filing Date: 2023-01-27).
*   **Offset: Massive Backlog & Defense Diversification.** The record $520 billion commercial backlog (2024-02-13) provides long-term revenue visibility. Recent defense contract wins (e.g., 2024-03-27) demonstrate ongoing government business.
*   **Offset: Management Restructuring.** The announced leadership changes (2024-03-26) are viewed by some analysts as a potential turning point to address operational issues.
*   **Offset: Cyclical Demand.** Commentary suggests a potential rebound in commercial aviation demand and a "rebound is inevitable" view from some market participants (2024-03-14), though this is speculative.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Cautiously Negative.**

The franchise remains fundamentally important in aerospace with a substantial backlog and diversified operations. However, the point-in-time narrative is dominated by severe operational and reputational crises stemming from the January 2024 incident. The breadth of ongoing FAA investigations, civil lawsuits, and documented quality control allegations presents material, ongoing risks to production, certification, and financial performance. While a management overhaul and a strong backlog provide potential offsets, the current trajectory is defined by regulatory and legal overhang with no clear near-term resolution, overshadowing the underlying business position.

**Confirmerd Evidence vs. Inference:** All dates, events (management change, contract, lawsuits), and the backlog figure are confirmed from the supplied text. The assessment of crisis severity and its primacy over other factors is an inference drawn from the volume and weight of the negative developments in the provided evidence window.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Equity has been negative since at least Q1 2022 (e.g., -$15.27B in Q1 2022)' — the raw pack confirms this with total_equity=-15.27B on 2022-03-31, so the claim is supported. However, the report also says 'Net margin has been negative across all quarters' — the raw income statement shows Q2 2022 net_income=+160.00M (positive), so this is factually incorrect for that quarter. The TTM net margin is negative, but the blanket 'all quarters' claim is wrong.
corrections.required_action: Correct the statement to note that net income was positive in Q2 2022 (EPS=+0.32), while acknowledging TTM and most recent quarters are negative.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites 'record backlog of $520 billion' from a 2024-02-13 web context source. This is a third-party summary of Boeing's 10-K, not a direct filing. The 10-K business section provided in the raw evidence (filing_date=2023-01-27, period_end=2022-12-31) does not contain a backlog figure. The backlog claim is sourced from a web context item, which is within the supplied evidence, but the PM should note this is a third-party report, not a primary filing in the pack.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed — the source is within supplied evidence. Note for PM that the $520B backlog figure comes from a third-party summary of the 2023 10-K, not from the 10-K text provided in the raw pack.

corrections.issue: Qual report states '$1.6 billion in additional losses were booked on fixed-price contracts' from the same 2024-02-13 web context. This is material negative evidence that the quant report does not reference. The quant report's downside section mentions 'no cash flow data raise liquidity concerns' but does not flag the $1.6B in additional fixed-price contract losses, which directly impacts earnings trajectory and balance-sheet risk.
corrections.required_action: PM should note the $1.6B fixed-price contract losses as a material earnings-quality risk not captured in the quant report's downside analysis.

unsupported_claims: Quant report: 'Net margin has been negative across all quarters' — contradicted by raw income statement showing Q2 2022 net_income=+160.00M (positive EPS of +0.32).

Quant report: 'improving net margins (from -0.076 to -0.029)' — the sparse history in the raw pack shows net_margin went from -0.076 (2023-04-03) to -0.029 (2024-03-28), which is directionally correct. However, the report frames this as an 'upside' driver without noting that the improvement is entirely from deeply negative levels and the margin remains negative, making the framing somewhat misleading in isolation.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's franchise value and $520B backlog provide long-term optionality, but the stock at $192.99 has already corrected -26.4% from its Q4 2023 highs amid a cascade of quality crises, leadership turnover, and persistent negative equity of -$17.23B. Risk/reward is roughly balanced: the selloff has priced in substantial near-term headwinds, but the path to earnings normalization remains uncertain given TTM losses of -$2.24B, $1.6B in additional fixed-price contract losses, and unresolved FAA investigations.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's franchise value and $520B backlog provide long-term optionality, but the stock at $192.99 has already corrected -26.4% from its Q4 2023 highs amid a cascade of quality crises, leadership turnover, and persistent negative equity of -$17.23B. Risk/reward is roughly balanced: the selloff has priced in substantial near-term headwinds, but the path to earnings normalization remains uncertain given TTM losses of -$2.24B, $1.6B in additional fixed-price contract losses, and unresolved FAA investigations.
key_factors: Revenue growth remains solid with Q4 2023 at $22.02B (+10.2% YoY, +21.6% QoQ) and a record $520B commercial backlog providing multi-year visibility, though this is sourced from a third-party summary of the 2023 10-K.

Balance sheet is severely impaired with negative equity of -$17.23B, total debt of $52.05B, and TTM net losses of -$2.24B; the $1.6B in additional fixed-price contract losses (per 2024-02-13 report) further pressures earnings trajectory.

The January 5th door plug incident triggered comprehensive FAA audits, multiple shareholder and passenger lawsuits, and a full management overhaul including CEO departure (2024-03-26), creating material regulatory and reputational overhang with no clear near-term resolution.

Price has corrected -26.4% QoQ to $192.99 (near 52-week low of $176.25), partially reflecting the crisis, while net margins have improved from -7.6% to -2.9% over the past year — though they remain deeply negative and the improvement is from a very low base.

Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date they are made. We undertake no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements, whether because of new information, future events, or otherwise.
3
Item 1
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
Embracing Our Future
Microsoft is a technology company whose mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. We strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world. We are creating the platforms and tools, powered by artificial intelligence ("AI"), that deliver better, faster, and more effective solutions to support small and large business competitiveness, improve educational and health outcomes, grow public-sector efficiency,  and empower human ingenuity. From infrastructure and data, to business applications and collaboration, we provide unique, differentiated value to customers.
In a world of increasing economic complexity, AI has the power to revolutionize many types of work. Microsoft is now innovating and expanding our portfolio with AI capabilities to help people and organizations overcome today's challenges and emerge stronger. Customers 
…[truncated 19275 chars]…
ing, and inclusive work environment that enables our global employees to create products and services that further our mission.
8
Item 1
As of June 30, 2023, we employed approximately 221,000 people on a full-time basis, 120,000 in the U.S. and 101,000 internationally. Of the total employed people, 89,000 were in operations, including manufacturing, distribution, product support, and consulting services; 72,000 were in product research and development; 45,000 were in sales and marketing; and 15,000 were in general and administration. Certain employees are subject to collective bargaining agreements.
Our Culture
Microsoft's culture is grounded in growth mindset. This means everyone is on a continuous journey to learn and grow, operating as one company instead of multiple siloed businesses.
Our employee listening systems enable us to gather feedback directly from our workforce to inform our programs and employee needs globally. Employees participate in our Employee Signals surveys, which cover a variety of topics such as thriving, inclusion, team culture, wellbeing, and learning and development. We also collect Daily Signals employee survey responses, giving us real-time insights into ways we can support our employees. In addition to Employee Signals and Daily Signals surveys, we gain insights through onboarding, exit surveys, internal Viva Engage channels, employee Q&A sessions, and our internal AskHR Service support.
Diversity and inclusion are core to our business model, and we hold ourselves accountable for driving global systemic change in our workforce and creating an inclusive work environment. We support multiple highly active Employee Resource Groups for women, families, racial and ethnic minorities, military, people with disabilities, and employees who identify as LGBTQIA+, where employees can go for support, networking, and community-building. As described in our 2022 Proxy Statement, annual performance and compensation reviews of our senior leadership team include an evaluation of their contributions to employee culture and diversity. To ensure accountability over time, we publicly disclose our progress on a multitude of workforce metrics including:
- Detailed breakdowns of gender, racial, and ethnic minority representation in our employee population, with data by job types, levels, and segments of our business.
- Our EEO-1 report (equal employment opportunity).
- Disability representation.
- Pay equity (see details below).
Total Rewards and Pay Equity
We develop dynamic, sustainable, market-driven, and strategic programs with the goal of providing a highly differentiated portfolio to attract, reward, and retain top talent and enable our employees to thrive. These programs reinforce our culture and values such as collaboration and growth mindset. Managers evaluate and recommend rewards based on, for example, how well we leverage the work of others and contribute to the success of our colleagues. We monitor pay equity and career progress across multiple dimensions. Our total compensation opportunity is highly differentiated and is market competitive.
In order to manage our costs in a dynamic, competitive environment, in fiscal year 2023 we announced that base salaries of salaried employees would remain at fiscal year 2022 levels. Pay increases continue to be available for rewards-eligible hourly and equivalent employees. We will continue our practice of investing in stock for all rewards-eligible employees, salaried and hourly, and investing in bonuses for all eligible employees.
9
Item 1
Since 2016, we have reported on pay equity as part of our annual Diversity and Inclusion report. In 2022, we reported that all racial and ethnic minority employees in the U.S. combined earn $1.008 for every $1.000 earned by their white counterparts, that women in the U.S. earn $1.007 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts who are men, and that women outside the U.S. earn $1.002 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts outside the U.S. who are men. In this year's report, we again expanded our pay equity data beyond the U.S. to report on 61 additional countries (up from 12 last year), representing 99.8% of our global Microsoft workforce.
In addition, we began reporting on unadjusted median pay in our annual report, comparing total pay amounts for all employees regardless of factors such as job title, level, or tenure. For employees who are eligible for rewards, the analysis showed that total pay for women is 89.6% of total pay for men in the U.S. and 86.2% outside of the U.S., and total pay for racial and ethnic minorities in the U.S. is 89.9% of total pay for white employees. As we continue to increase representation for women and racial and ethnic minorities at more senior levels, and continue to ensure pay equity for all, the gap between the medians will reduce.
Our intended result is a global performance and development approach that fosters our culture, and competitive compensation that ensures equitable pay by role while supporting pay for performance.
Wellbeing and Hybrid Work
Microsoft is committed to supporting our employees' wellbeing while they are at work and in their personal lives. We have invested significantly in wellbeing, and offer a differentiated benefits package which includes many physical, emotional, and financial wellness programs including counseling through the Microsoft CARES Employee Assistance Program, mental wellbeing support, flexible fitness benefits, disability accommodations, savings and investment tools, adoption assistance, and back-up care for children and elders. Finally, our Occupational Health and Safety program helps ensure employees can stay safe while they are working.
We introduced Hybrid Workplace Flexibility Guidance to better support leaders, managers, and employees in hybrid work scenarios. Our ongoing survey data shows that 93% of employees value the flexibility related to work location, work site, and work hours, and 78% are satisfied with the quality of connection with co-workers. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to flexible work at Microsoft. As a company, we will continue to leverage data and research to inform decision making, balancing the needs of business, team, and individual.
Learning and Development
We offer a range of learning opportunities, including personalized opportunities on our internal and external learning portals, in-classroom learning, required learning on compliance and company culture, on-the-job advancement opportunities, and manager coaching. We also provide customized manager learning, new employee orientation, and tools for operating in a flexible hybrid work environment.
All Microsoft employees globally access our single Viva Learning tool for both required and personal choice learning. This includes courses focused on our core principles and compliance matters, such as Business Conduct, Privacy, Security Foundations, and Harassment Prevention. We also deliver skills training for employees based on their profession and role discipline.
We have over 27,000 people managers, all of whom must complete between 20-33 hours of compulsory training on leadership and management and are assigned additional targeted training on an ongoing basis related to people management, compliance, and culture.
10
Item 1
OPERATING SEGMENTS
We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses. The segments enable the alignment of strategies and objectives across the development, sales, marketing, and services organizations, and they provide a framework for timely and rational allocation of resources within businesses.
